steps.title: Name the two moves
steps.content: The underlined portion first considers a tempting interpretation: that fast painting explains the ethereal qualities associated with the Highwaymen aesthetic. It then rejects that interpretation as inaccurate because Hair's methods were not universally practiced by the artists connected to him.

steps.title: Use the counterexample
steps.content: The next part supplies Roy McLendon as evidence: he painted more deliberately but still achieved the same effects. That means the underlined portion is considering and rebutting an interpretation of a technique's effect. A invents a disagreement, B reverses who originated the method, and D invents a central-versus-peripheral contrast. The answer is C.
